sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: KAA Gent are struggling, winless in their last three matches with only one victory in their past five (1W-2D-2L), conceding in all of them. KVC Westerlo, on the other hand, are in fine form, unbeaten in their last five matches (2W-3D), including a strong 4-0 win over Anderlecht. This momentum gives Westerlo a slight edge heading into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, matches between KAA Gent and KVC Westerlo have been tight, ending in draws (0-0 and 2-2). Historically, Gent dominate with no losses in 11 meetings, and both teams often score, with 8 of the last 10 games seeing goals from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Gent, Omri Gandelman (7 goals, 1 assist) is a major threat, while Davy Roef’s goalkeeping (7.64 avg. rating last 5 matches) could be crucial. Westerlo’s Nacho Ferri (5 goals, 2 assists) and Doğan Haspolat (43 key passes, league top 5) will look to exploit Gent’s defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Gent rank 7th in goals scored (26) but struggle defensively (29 conceded, 11th in league), while Westerlo have a balanced attack and defense (28 scored, 28 conceded). Gent’s strength in aerial duels (8 headers scored, league best) contrasts with Westerlo’s knack for late goals (28.57% in 76-90 min).

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Despite Sofascore’s close prediction (39% home win, 37% away win, 24% draw), Westerlo’s current form and Gent’s defensive issues tilt the balance slightly in favor of the visitors. Expect a competitive game with goals likely on both ends, given recent trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between KAA Gent and KVC Westerlo at the Planet Group Arena ended with a comfortable 2-0 lead for the home side. Gent capitalized on their attacking opportunities with goals from Hyllarion Goore and Maksim Paskotsi, showcasing their clinical finishing. Westerlo, despite having more ball possession and a higher number of final third entries, failed to convert their chances into goals. The away side created fewer big chances and missed a key opportunity, which has left them trailing at the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The attack momentum in the first half saw significant swings between both teams. Initially, Westerlo started with strong attacking intent, dominating the early minutes with consistent pressure. However, Gent gradually took control around the midway point of the half, with notable attacking spells that coincided with their two goals. Their momentum peaked during key moments, especially around the times of their strikes. Towards the end of the half, Westerlo attempted to regain some footing with renewed pressure, but Gent largely maintained their attacking presence through sustained periods of dominance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player performances
halftimeSections.text: For KAA Gent, Maksim Paskotsi stood out with a goal and a commanding defensive display, earning high praise for his overall contribution. Hyllarion Goore also made a significant impact by finding the back of the net early on. Atsuki Ito was pivotal in midfield, providing an assist and creating a big chance. On the other side, KVC Westerlo's Nacho Ferri showed promise upfront with a couple of efforts on target, despite missing a big chance. Isa Sakamoto contributed creatively by setting up a key opportunity, but overall, Westerlo lacked the finishing touch to challenge Gent's defense effectively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team statistics breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Looking at the team statistics, KAA Gent made the most of their opportunities, creating more big chances and converting them into goals, despite having slightly less possession than Westerlo. Their shots on target were more effective, and they showed intent with numerous penalty area entries. Westerlo, however, dominated possession and excelled in duels, particularly in the air. They also recorded a high number of final third entries, indicating their persistence in attack, but their expected goals were significantly lower than Gent's, reflecting a lack of quality in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, KAA Gent will likely look to protect their two-goal cushion while seeking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities. Given their efficiency in front of goal in the first half, they may adopt a more measured approach to conserve energy. KVC Westerlo, on the other hand, must push forward with greater urgency to overturn the deficit. They have shown they can penetrate the final third, but will need to improve their finishing and capitalize on any chances created. Expect Westerlo to take more risks, potentially leaving spaces at the back for Gent to exploit. A goal for the away side could shift the momentum, but Gent's defensive solidity might prove difficult to break down.
